title: Does Private-Credit Smoke Mean Financial Fire?
content: Does the rapid growth in non-bank lending pose a threat to the stability of the financial system? While the current chair of the US Securities and Exchange Commission insists that it does not, there are growing signs that something is rotten in the state of private credit.

creator: Howard Davies
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Will AI Solve Rich Countries’ Debt Woes?
content: Although many commentators believe that AI has the potential to supercharge economic growth and solve our most vexing problems, such optimism underplays the risks. If anything, the technology is likely to cause profound—and costly—problems on the way to the halcyon future.
snippet: Although many commentators believe that AI has the potential to supercharge economic growth and solve our most vexing problems, such optimism underplays the risks. If anything, the technology is likel
creator: Kenneth Rogoff
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Escaping the IMF Austerity Trap
content: There is ample research showing that growth-oriented strategies accompanied by redistribution policies are the most promising path to recovery across the Global South. Rather than imposing cookie-cutter austerity programs, the International Monetary Fund should be following the evidence.
snippet: There is ample research showing that growth-oriented strategies accompanied by redistribution policies are the most promising path to recovery across the Global South. Rather than imposing cookie-cutt
creator: Kevin P. Gallagher
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Decoupling and South Korea’s New Capitalism
content: The South Korean stock market’s remarkable recent performance reflects not only successful government initiatives but also the rapid rise of individual investors. Now, instead of always reinvesting profits, Korean firms are delivering more short-term value to shareholders—a change that could help keep capital in the country.
snippet: The South Korean stock market’s remarkable recent performance reflects not only successful government initiatives but also the rapid rise of individual investors. Now, instead of always reinvesting pr
creator: Keun Lee
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: American Jews’ Netanyahu Problem
content: Political polarization, the collapse of institutional gatekeepers, and social media’s amplification of fringe voices were always going to put pressure on American Jews. But Benjamin Netanyahu's alliances and policies have compounded these forces, putting US Jews in the crossfire of partisan politics and Middle East adventurism.
snippet: Political polarization, the collapse of institutional gatekeepers, and social media’s amplification of fringe voices were always going to put pressure on American Jews. But Benjamin Netanyahu's allian
creator: Steven Simon
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: To Strengthen Climate Resilience, Focus on Social Protection
content: The international community is increasingly trying to distinguish between climate, development, and humanitarian finance—as if they can be neatly compartmentalized. But this siloed approach overlooks how social-protection programs providing cash transfers to vulnerable households can strengthen resilience to climate shocks.
snippet: The international community is increasingly trying to distinguish between climate, development, and humanitarian finance—as if they can be neatly compartmentalized. But this siloed approach overlooks
creator: Ana Toni
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Deafening Silence on Offshore Wealth
content: The kind of hidden wealth that the Panama Papers exposed a decade ago fuels corruption, distorts markets, drives inequality, finances authoritarian regimes, and weakens democracies. Yet it endures—not because it is inevitable, but because powerful interests benefit from it.
snippet: The kind of hidden wealth that the Panama Papers exposed a decade ago fuels corruption, distorts markets, drives inequality, finances authoritarian regimes, and weakens democracies. Yet it endures—not
creator: Frederik Obermaier
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Risky Geography of the Cloud
content: Iran's recent drone strikes on Gulf states mark the first time commercial data centers have been targeted in an organized fashion. Europe, especially, should reflect on the assumptions underlying digital-infrastructure investments and deployments, because attacks on data centers in the Middle East can jeopardize its own economic security.
snippet: Iran's recent drone strikes on Gulf states mark the first time commercial data centers have been targeted in an organized fashion. Europe, especially, should reflect on the assumptions underlying digi
creator: Soňa Muzikárová
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump’s Self-Defeating Attacks on the Fed
content: Instead of quietly winding down the meritless investigation of US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, the Trump administration has ramped it up, with prosecutors showing up unannounced at the Fed’s construction site. But the president's political charade will only delay the confirmation of Kevin Warsh as Powell’s successor.
snippet: Instead of quietly winding down the meritless investigation of US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, the Trump administration has ramped it up, with prosecutors showing up unannounced at the Fed’s c
creator: Michael R. Strain
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The World After Trump
content: The international economic order that has prevailed since the end of World War II is gone for good. With the United States abdicating its leadership responsibilities, it falls to middle powers to construct a system that accommodates national preferences while safeguarding global stability.
snippet: The international economic order that has prevailed since the end of World War II is gone for good. With the United States abdicating its leadership responsibilities, it falls to middle powers to cons
creator: George Papaconstantinou
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Geopolitics of Infrastructure
content: No longer does global power rest only on alliances, military might, currency dynamics, and effective control of multilateral institutions. The new geopolitical contest is between competing infrastructure blocs: packages of finance, contractors, standards, and data systems that create long-term dependencies.
snippet: No longer does global power rest only on alliances, military might, currency dynamics, and effective control of multilateral institutions. The new geopolitical contest is between competing infrastruct
creator: Bertrand Badré
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Africa’s Geopolitical Hand Is Stronger than Ever
content: As the old world order fractures and a multipolar era takes shape, Africa has an unprecedented opportunity to translate its global position and enviable economic endowments into durable geopolitical and economic leverage. But African leaders will have to play their cards right.
snippet: As the old world order fractures and a multipolar era takes shape, Africa has an unprecedented opportunity to translate its global position and enviable economic endowments into durable geopolitical a
creator: Xolisa Ngwadla
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Private Credit Panic Is Overblown
content: Private credit markets are under growing stress, fueling fears of a financial crisis that could spill over to the real economy. But a closer look at the evidence suggests that the risks are less severe than in previous cycles, and that predictions of a meltdown are running well ahead of the facts.
snippet: Private credit markets are under growing stress, fueling fears of a financial crisis that could spill over to the real economy. But a closer look at the evidence suggests that the risks are less sever
creator: Dambisa Moyo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Hormuz Today, Taiwan Tomorrow
content: By closing the Strait of Hormuz, Iran has handed the Trump administration a practice test. To pass—and preserve deterrence against a Chinese invasion or blockade of Taiwan—the United States must reopen the Strait decisively and visibly with escorts, minesweepers, and strikes on launch sites.
snippet: By closing the Strait of Hormuz, Iran has handed the Trump administration a practice test. To pass—and preserve deterrence against a Chinese invasion or blockade of Taiwan—the United States must reope
creator: Todd G. Buchholz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Really Drives China’s Massive Trade Surplus
content: China’s trade surplus is often blamed on its industrial policies. In reality, however, it reflects a persistent gap between savings and investment, driven by demographic pressures and financial constraints that shape household behavior and restrict private firms’ access to credit.
snippet: China’s trade surplus is often blamed on its industrial policies. In reality, however, it reflects a persistent gap between savings and investment, driven by demographic pressures and financial constr
creator: Shang-Jin Wei
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The World Needs an Oil Buyers’ Club
content: As the world is plunged into another energy crisis, market allocation is leading to grossly unjust outcomes, as the rich outbid the poor. A multilateral oil buyers' club is urgently needed to defend a price ceiling in global oil markets and allocate resources in a way that meets people’s essential needs and minimizes the economic fallout.
snippet: As the world is plunged into another energy crisis, market allocation is leading to grossly unjust outcomes, as the rich outbid the poor. A multilateral oil buyers' club is urgently needed to defend a
creator: Isabella M. Weber
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A New Security Architecture for the Middle East
content: The tense negotiations between the United States and Iran have exposed the limits of bilateral diplomacy. With the crisis fueled by overlapping, interconnected conflicts, the only viable path forward is a broader regional framework that addresses the Strait of Hormuz, nuclear proliferation, Palestinian statehood, and proxy warfare.
snippet: The tense negotiations between the United States and Iran have exposed the limits of bilateral diplomacy. With the crisis fueled by overlapping, interconnected conflicts, the only viable path forward
creator: Ahmet Davutoğlu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Fossil-Fuel Investments Are a Fiduciary Risk
content: The Iran war has reminded everyone, but especially Africans, of the structural instability of fossil-fuel prices. For African trustees, directors, asset managers, and other fiduciaries, the question is not whether capital should reposition, but whether institutions will act before events compel them to do so.
snippet: The Iran war has reminded everyone, but especially Africans, of the structural instability of fossil-fuel prices. For African trustees, directors, asset managers, and other fiduciaries, the question i
creator: Nicole Martens
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe’s Digital Decade Is in Disarray
content: Under the banner of the European Union’s Digital Decade agenda, Europe is investing in digitalization to protect its industries from shocks like the one currently emanating from the Middle East. But if EU leaders think their current program is sufficient, they are in for a rude awakening.
snippet: Under the banner of the European Union’s Digital Decade agenda, Europe is investing in digitalization to protect its industries from shocks like the one currently emanating from the Middle East. But i
creator: Laura Mahrenbach
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A European Populist Defends an American Pope
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's response to Donald Trump's attacks on Pope Leo XIV may strain her relationship with the White House, but it is likely to help her domestically. Remarkably, this outcome materialized in a staunch, if unwitting, defense of European values.
snippet: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's response to Donald Trump's attacks on Pope Leo XIV may strain her relationship with the White House, but it is likely to help her domestically. Remarkably, this
creator: Fabrizio Tassinari
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump Has Made the Case for International Law
content: By taking a wrecking ball to the global rules-based order, Donald Trump has thrown the entire world into chaos, reminding us why our post-World War II forebears laid the foundation for that order. The task now is to re-imagine an international legal regime that works more reliably for more people, without exception for the powerful.
snippet: By taking a wrecking ball to the global rules-based order, Donald Trump has thrown the entire world into chaos, reminding us why our post-World War II forebears laid the foundation for that order. The
creator: James A. Goldston
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Adam Smith on the US Midterms
content: The Wealth of Nations offers a useful lens for understanding why US President Donald Trump’s mercantilist agenda has fallen short of its own stated goals. It also points to a better path, combining competitive markets with policies that help workers and communities build skills and keep pace with economic change.
snippet: The Wealth of Nations offers a useful lens for understanding why US President Donald Trump’s mercantilist agenda has fallen short of its own stated goals. It also points to a better path, combining co
creator: Glenn Hubbard
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is a Perfect Financial Storm Gathering?
content: Between the explosive growth of shadow banking, the relentless rise in public debt across major economies, and stalled implementation of the regulatory reforms enacted after the 2008 financial crisis, those charged with maintaining financial stability have much reason to worry. The window to do something about it is closing fast.
snippet: Between the explosive growth of shadow banking, the relentless rise in public debt across major economies, and stalled implementation of the regulatory reforms enacted after the 2008 financial crisis,
creator: Agustín Carstens
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Morocco’s New Model Economy
content: Africa’s future will not be written by countries waiting for perfect conditions. It will be written by countries like Morocco, which made the most of its undesirable post-colonial inheritance to become not only an economic success story but a potential model for others to emulate.
snippet: Africa’s future will not be written by countries waiting for perfect conditions. It will be written by countries like Morocco, which made the most of its undesirable post-colonial inheritance to becom
creator: Abdelmalek Alaoui
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The West Is Still Getting Russia Wrong
content: While Vladimir Putin views Russia as a great power and civilizational counterweight to Western liberalism, he does not have a coherent plan to remake the world, let alone the capacity to do so. Because of this weakness, the Kremlin is focused not on domination, but on disruption.
snippet: While Vladimir Putin views Russia as a great power and civilizational counterweight to Western liberalism, he does not have a coherent plan to remake the world, let alone the capacity to do so. Becaus
creator: Inna Bondarenko
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Global AI Threat Has Arrived
content: The ability of Anthropic’s new AI model, Claude Mythos Preview, to find vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers has dangerous implications for today’s highly interconnected world. Instead of merely securing its own firms, the United States must address the problem through diplomacy, particularly with China.
